close_reason: Added is_credential_misconfiguration() that stops chain on config errors instead of falling through
description:
src/lib.rs:215-220 ChainedCredential::get_token tries each source in order and returns the first Ok. The crate doc warns about MI-vs-developer fallback in production, but the *default* CredentialMode is Chained — meaning by default, a production deployment whose managed identity is misconfigured will silently authenticate via developer tools (if azure-cli happens to be installed) or fall through to a confusing terminal error.

Trigger: production deployment with mistyped MI assignment, missing role, etc.

Maintenance consequence: operators see no signal that MI is broken. When the dev token eventually expires or the dev tools are removed in a hardened image, the system fails in production with no breadcrumb trail. Worse, the dev fallback may have different RBAC than MI, leading to silent permission divergence.

Fix options: (a) on Chained, log a warning the first time a non-MI source produces the token; (b) make ManagedIdentity the default and require explicit chained=true; (c) capture and surface the MI error as a structured warning in the returned token (no API exists for this in TokenCredential, so (a) or (b) are the realistic options).
title: Chained credential mode silently masks managed-identity misconfiguration
